arts.ttl: Jamie Carragher and Emmanuel Petit noticed the same thing about Chelsea's supporters in 3-1 win over Wolves, it needs to change
arts.sum: Chelsea fans will no doubt have left Stamford Bridge relieved following their 3-1 win over Wolves tonight. The Blues returned to the top four with...

Chelsea moved back into the top four of the Premier League with a 3-1 win against Wolves. Here’s how we rated each Blues player. Robert Sanchez: 5 Gifted Wolves an equaliser by dropping a corner at the feet of Matt Doherty at the end of the first half. He did make decent saves from Matheus […] The post Chelsea v Wolves player ratings appeared first on West London Sport.

arts.ttl: Chelsea 3-1 Wolves: Player Ratings as Blues Win First Premier League Game of 2025
arts.sum: Chelsea dispatched Wolves 3–1 and returned to winning ways in the Premier League.

arts.ttl: Alejandro Garnacho agents spotted at Stamford Bridge amid Chelsea transfer links
arts.sum: The agents of Manchester United forward Alejandro Garnacho were at Stamford Bridge for Chelsea's Premier League clash against Wolverhampton Wanderers on Monday night. He needs to learn to play in a different position.

arts.ttl: Premier League release statement after Tosin Adarabioyo goal for Chelsea overruled by VAR
arts.sum: The Blues were facing Wolves in the Monday Night Football clash in the Premier League at Stamford Bridge and went into the game in the back of a draw with Bournemouth in their previous fixture. The Premier League have explained why Tosin Adarabioyo's goal for Chelsea against Wolves was allowed to stand.

arts.ttl: Chelsea submit to £145m transfer reality as Caicedo injury worry laid bare
arts.sum: Other than losing perhaps their most important and irreplaceable player (Cole Palmer is right to warrant a word but Chelsea have attacking depth and severely lack it elsewhere), there was a bigger issue at play. Caicedo was and continues to be running on fumes, whilst Dewsbury-Hall has simply not had the game time to be able to find any sort of sharpness.

arts.ttl: Chelsea vs Wolves highlights: Goals from Tosin, Cucurella and Madueke amid Sanchez howler
arts.sum: With a trip to Manchester City next up after Wolves, Chelsea know that failing to win their first home league game in over a month would raise serious questions of the head coach. After an extra few days of rest, this is a big game for Enzo Maresca's side.

arts.ttl: Why Enzo Maresca was unhappy with Tyrique George and Pedro Neto Chelsea disappointment vs Wolves
arts.sum: Moments later Chelsea put the game out of sight as Madueke helped himself to yet another goal against Wolves after converting from practically on the line just as Trevoh Chalobah's header from Cole Palmer's free-kick looked set to go in. The hosts were to restore their lead on the hour mark as Marc Cucurella was in the right position to convert in the six-yard box from Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall's flicked header from Noni Madueke's cross.

arts.ttl: Chelsea player ratings as Palmer quiet but Madueke turns it on in Wolves win
arts.sum: Marc Cucurella got on the end of a Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall flick on to score his third goal in eight matches in all competitions for the west Londoners. Swiftly after that, Chelsea went two goals ahead with Noni Madueke headed in from close range after a fine header from Trevoh Chalobah.

arts.ttl: Chelsea’s surprise ‘signing’ Trevoh Chalobah offers versatility to regain momentum with Wolves win
arts.sum: Chelsea 3-1 Wolves: The Blues ended a five-game streak without a win with Chalobah shining for the hosts after being recalled from his loan spell at Crystal Palace
